name: Suffolk Trifle (English)
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the macaroons in a glass bowl and pour over the wine and brandy.
2. Leave the macaroons to soak, adding extra wine or brandy if they appear dry.
3. Pour the single cream into a pan and warm gently until just on the point of boiling.
4. Beat together the egg, egg yolk, and cornflour in a bowl, pour onto the cream, and whisk lightly.
5. Return to a clean saucepan and cook gently, without boiling, until custard has thickened, stirring continuously.
6. Stir in the sugar and allow the custard to cool slightly.
7. Pour over the soaked macaroons and set in a cool place.
8. Carefully smooth the jam over the custard, then sprinkle with almonds and candied peel.
9. Whip the double cream until it stands in soft peaks and pipe or pile on top of trifle.
10. Serve decorated with extra blanched almonds and candied peel, if desired.
